pattern: Each diametral cut produces two equal slices, so an even count needs half as many cuts, an odd count needs one cut per slice, and a single slice needs none.
hints: Think about how many slices one straight cut through the center can create, then how that changes when the number of slices you want is odd versus even.
A cut through the center spans the whole circle, so it yields two slices at once only when the target slice count is even.
Handle n equal to 1 as zero cuts; for even n return n divided by 2; for odd n greater than 1 return n.

edge_inputs.description: two slices from one diametral cut

stress.note: n at most 100, reference is constant time
